title: Eye Health. Impacts of Regular Consumption of Grapes on Macular Pigment Accumulation in Singapore Older Adults: A Randomized, Controlled Trial. In a randomized, controlled human study, 34 human subjects consumed either grapes, equivalent to 1.5 cups of grapes per day, or a placebo for 16 weeks. Those consuming grapes showed a significant increase in Macular Pigment Optical Density, plasma antioxidant capacity, and total phenolic content compared to those on placebo. Those who did not consume grapes experienced a significant increase in harmful ocular advanced glycation end products.

sentences.text: Impacts of Regular Consumption of Grapes on Macular Pigment Accumulation in Singapore Older Adults: A Randomized, Controlled Trial.
In a randomized, controlled human study, 34 human subjects consumed either grapes, equivalent to 1.5 cups of grapes per day, or a placebo for 16 weeks.
Those consuming grapes showed a significant increase in Macular Pigment Optical Density, plasma antioxidant capacity, and total phenolic content compared to those on placebo.
Those who did not consume grapes experienced a significant increase in harmful ocular advanced glycation end products.
A Grape-Supplemented Diet Prevented Ultraviolet Radiation-Induced Cataract by Regulating Nrf2 and XIAP Pathways.
This study examined the impact of grape consumption for 3 months against ultraviolet-induced cataract formation in mice.
Animals were fed a diet comprised of 5, 10, or 15 percent grape powder.
The results showed that adding grapes to the diet dose-dependently inhibited cataract formation and preserved glutathione pools.
The 15 percent grape diet most significantly inhibited the onset and severity of cataracts, resulting in clear lenses.
Additionally, ultraviolet-induced Nrf2 activation was abolished in groups on the grape diet, suggesting improved redox homeostasis in the lens.
Protective Effects of a Grape Supplemented Diet in a Mouse Model of Retinal Degeneration.
In this study, adding freeze-dried grape powder to the diet preserved retinal structure and function compared to a sugar-matched control.
In addition, retinal thickness and photoreceptor numbers were preserved by the grape-supplemented diet compared to controls.
The Sustained Delivery of Resveratrol or a Defined Grape Powder Inhibits New Blood Vessel Formation in a Mouse Model of Choroidal Neovascularization.

In a controlled feeding trial with mice, provision of grape powder significantly reduced the formation of harmful blood vessels, choroidal neovascularization, associated with models of the wet form of age-related macular degeneration, a leading cause of blindness in the industrialized world.
Researchers found that grape consumption activated the notch signaling pathway to reduce pathological angiogenesis.
